From the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} onward it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Satake clan|Q2975363|WD=Q2975363}}. According to this shrine's origin record, {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu (Iyo-no-kami)|Q11383430|WD=Q11383430|lt=Satake Yoshinobu}} repaired the buildings in the {{ill|lt=Shōhei|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei}} era, and shrine tradition says {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshitoshi|Q11383467|WD=Q11383467}} moved it to the current location in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299}}). In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454}}), {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850}}: from 10th merit rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower, 10th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}) - written as "Sato deity". * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}), {{ill|lt=May 27|Q2587|WD=Q2587}}: from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}) - written as "Sato deity". * Jōgan 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843}}), {{ill|lt=December 29|Q2862|WD=Q2862}}: from Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Sato deity". === Precincts === File:Sato Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sato Jinja Keidai.jpg|Shrine marker and precincts File:Sato Atago Jinja.jpg|Atago Shrine torii === References === * Explanatory board in the precincts * "Engishiki shrine of Kuji District, Ninomiya Sato Shrine" (origin text) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province|Q11481683|WD=Q11481683}} {{神道 横}}{{Index of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663}}Satonomiya-chō 1052 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721}}) |本殿 = Hiyoshi-zukuri, copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Sato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sato Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sato Shrine''' (さとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deity === * '''Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto''' (たちはやびおのみこと) This deity appears in the {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687}} as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}}. The text says: "His name is '''Tachihayao-no-Mikoto'''; another name is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayafuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'''." In the fudoki, when local residents suffered from the deity's curse, Kataoka no Ōmuraji (grandson of {{ill|lt=Kunimo Ōkashima-no-Mikoto|Q99517269|WD=Q99517269}}), ancestor of the Kashima branch of the Nakatomi clan ({{ill|lt=Kashima clan|Q11677139|WD=Q11677139|lt=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji}}), was dispatched by the court to perform rites. === History === === Foundation === The {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687}} contains an entry on Sato Shrine and states that the founding was in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721}}), when a shrine was built in Matsuzawa. In Enryaku 19 ({{ill|lt=800|Q23677|WD=Q23677}}), villagers petitioned and a great minister was dispatched; an oracle said, "Rather than dwell in an impure village, settle in the pure heights." The shrine was moved to Kabire-no-mine (Iriyonoma-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachi|Q633922|WD=Q633922}}). In {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}), because the mountain was steep and worship difficult, it was moved again to Konakajima (Satonomiya-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663}}). === Overview ===
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;background-color:#ffffff;white-space: nowrap;text-align:center" |+Changes in divine ranks of Sato deity and Shizuka deity{{神道・神社史料集成|200601|Sato Shrine}}.{{神道・神社史料集成|201601|Shizuka Shrine}}. !Year!!Sato deity!!Shizuka deity |- |846||10th merit rank
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower||-- |- |866||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank||-- |- |874||Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank||-- |- |885||--||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Upper |- |{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}||Small||{{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} |}
The first appearance in official histories ({{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q11420601|WD=Q11420601}}) is in the entry for {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850}} of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454}}) in the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}, where Sato deity of 10th merit rank is granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. It continued to rise, reaching Junior Fourth Rank, Lower in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as a small shrine in {{ill|lt=Kuji District|Q1075212|WD=Q1075212}}, {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}. At the end of the Six National Histories period, its divine rank was higher than {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q11663648|WD=Q11663648}}, the district's {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}, but in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} it is listed as a small shrine, suggesting their relative influence reversed during that interval. From the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} onward it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Satake clan|Q2975363|WD=Q2975363}}. According to this shrine's origin record, {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu (Iyo-no-kami)|Q11383430|WD=Q11383430|lt=Satake Yoshinobu}} repaired the buildings in the {{ill|lt=Shōhei|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei}} era, and shrine tradition says {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshitoshi|Q11383467|WD=Q11383467}} moved it to the current location in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299}}). In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454}}), {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850}}: from 10th merit rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower, 10th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}) - written as "Sato deity". * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}), {{ill|lt=May 27|Q2587|WD=Q2587}}: from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}) - written as "Sato deity". * Jōgan 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843}}), {{ill|lt=December 29|Q2862|WD=Q2862}}: from Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Sato deity". === Precincts === File:Sato Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sato Jinja Keidai.jpg|Shrine marker and precincts File:Sato Atago Jinja.jpg|Atago Shrine torii === References === * Explanatory board in the precincts * "Engishiki shrine of Kuji District, Ninomiya Sato Shrine" (origin text) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province|Q11481683|WD=Q11481683}} {{神道 横}}{{Index of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663}}Satonomiya-chō 1052 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721}}) |本殿 = Hiyoshi-zukuri, copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Sato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sato Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sato Shrine''' (さとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deity === * '''Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto''' (たちはやびおのみこと) This deity appears in the {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687}} as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}}. The text says: "His name is '''Tachihayao-no-Mikoto'''; another name is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayafuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'''." In the fudoki, when local residents suffered from the deity's curse, Kataoka no Ōmuraji (grandson of {{ill|lt=Kunimo Ōkashima-no-Mikoto|Q99517269|WD=Q99517269}}), ancestor of the Kashima branch of the Nakatomi clan ({{ill|lt=Kashima clan|Q11677139|WD=Q11677139|lt=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji}}), was dispatched by the court to perform rites. === History === === Foundation === The {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687}} contains an entry on Sato Shrine and states that the founding was in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721}}), when a shrine was built in Matsuzawa. In Enryaku 19 ({{ill|lt=800|Q23677|WD=Q23677}}), villagers petitioned and a great minister was dispatched; an oracle said, "Rather than dwell in an impure village, settle in the pure heights." The shrine was moved to Kabire-no-mine (Iriyonoma-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachi|Q633922|WD=Q633922}}). In {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}), because the mountain was steep and worship difficult, it was moved again to Konakajima (Satonomiya-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663}}). === Overview ===
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;background-color:#ffffff;white-space: nowrap;text-align:center" |+Changes in divine ranks of Sato deity and Shizuka deity{{神道・神社史料集成|200601|Sato Shrine}}.{{神道・神社史料集成|201601|Shizuka Shrine}}. !Year!!Sato deity!!Shizuka deity |- |846||10th merit rank
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower||-- |- |866||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank||-- |- |874||Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank||-- |- |885||--||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Upper |- |{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}}||Small||{{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}} |}
The first appearance in official histories ({{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q11420601|WD=Q11420601}}) is in the entry for {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850}} of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454}}) in the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}, where Sato deity of 10th merit rank is granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. It continued to rise, reaching Junior Fourth Rank, Lower in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as a small shrine in {{ill|lt=Kuji District|Q1075212|WD=Q1075212}}, {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}. At the end of the Six National Histories period, its divine rank was higher than {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q11663648|WD=Q11663648}}, the district's {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}, but in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} it is listed as a small shrine, suggesting their relative influence reversed during that interval. From the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} onward it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Satake clan|Q2975363|WD=Q2975363}}. According to this shrine's origin record, {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu (Iyo-no-kami)|Q11383430|WD=Q11383430|lt=Satake Yoshinobu}} repaired the buildings in the {{ill|lt=Shōhei|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei}} era, and shrine tradition says {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshitoshi|Q11383467|WD=Q11383467}} moved it to the current location in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299}}). In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454}}), {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850}}: from 10th merit rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower, 10th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}) - written as "Sato deity". * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}), {{ill|lt=May 27|Q2587|WD=Q2587}}: from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}) - written as "Sato deity". * Jōgan 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843}}), {{ill|lt=December 29|Q2862|WD=Q2862}}: from Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Sato deity". === Precincts === File:Sato Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sato Jinja Keidai.jpg|Shrine marker and precincts File:Sato Atago Jinja.jpg|Atago Shrine torii === References === * Explanatory board in the precincts * "Engishiki shrine of Kuji District, Ninomiya Sato Shrine" (origin text) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province|Q11481683|WD=Q11481683}} {{神道 横}}{{Index of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

From the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} onward it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Satake clan|Q2975363|WD=Q2975363}}. In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454}}), {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850}}: from 10th merit rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower, 10th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}) - written as "Sato deity". * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}), {{ill|lt=May 27|Q2587|WD=Q2587}}: from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}) - written as "Sato deity". * Jōgan 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843}}), {{ill|lt=December 29|Q2862|WD=Q2862}}: from Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Sato deity". === Precincts === File:Sato Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sato Jinja Keidai.jpg|Shrine marker and precincts File:Sato Atago Jinja.jpg|Atago Shrine torii === References === * Explanatory board in the precincts * "Engishiki shrine of Kuji District, Ninomiya Sato Shrine" (origin text) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province|Q11481683|WD=Q11481683}} {{神道 横}}{{Index of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

|画像 = [[File:Sato Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}Satonomiya-chō 1052 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721|1=788}}) |本殿 = Hiyoshi-zukuri, copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Sato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sato Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sato Shrine''' (さとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * '''Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto''' (たちはやびおのみこと) This deity appears in the {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}}. The text says: "His name is '''Tachihayao-no-Mikoto'''; another name is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayafuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'''." In the fudoki, when local residents suffered from the deity's curse, Kataoka no Ōmuraji (grandson of {{ill|lt=Kunimo Ōkashima-no-Mikoto|Q99517269|WD=Q99517269|1=Kuninazu no Ōkashima no Mikoto}}), ancestor of the Kashima branch of the Nakatomi clan ({{ill|lt=Kashima clan|Q11677139|WD=Q11677139|lt=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji|1=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji}}), was dispatched by the court to perform rites. === History === === Foundation === The {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} contains an entry on Sato Shrine and states that the founding was in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721|1=788}}), when a shrine was built in Matsuzawa. In Enryaku 19 ({{ill|lt=800|Q23677|WD=Q23677|1=800}}), villagers petitioned and a great minister was dispatched; an oracle said, "Rather than dwell in an impure village, settle in the pure heights." The shrine was moved to Kabire-no-mine (Iriyonoma-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachi|Q633922|WD=Q633922|1=Hitachi, Ibaraki}}). In {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), because the mountain was steep and worship difficult, it was moved again to Konakajima (Satonomiya-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}). === Overview ===
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;background-color:#ffffff;white-space: nowrap;text-align:center" |+Changes in divine ranks of Sato deity and Shizuka deity{{神道・神社史料集成|200601|Sato Shrine}}.{{神道・神社史料集成|201601|Shizuka Shrine}}. !Year!!Sato deity!!Shizuka deity |- |846||10th merit rank
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower||-- |- |866||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank||-- |- |874||Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank||-- |- |885||--||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Upper |- |{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}||Small||{{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}} |}
The first appearance in official histories ({{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q11420601|WD=Q11420601|1=National Histories}}) is in the entry for {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}} of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}) in the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}, where Sato deity of 10th merit rank is granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. It continued to rise, reaching Junior Fourth Rank, Lower in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a small shrine in {{ill|lt=Kuji District|Q1075212|WD=Q1075212|1=Kuji District, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}. At the end of the Six National Histories period, its divine rank was higher than {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q11663648|WD=Q11663648|1=Shizu Shrine}}, the district's {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, but in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as a small shrine, suggesting their relative influence reversed during that interval. From the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Satake clan|Q2975363|WD=Q2975363|1=Satake clan}}. According to this shrine's origin record, {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu (Iyo-no-kami)|Q11383430|WD=Q11383430|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} repaired the buildings in the {{ill|lt=Shōhei|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} era, and shrine tradition says {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshitoshi|Q11383467|WD=Q11383467|1=Satake Yoshikiyo}} moved it to the current location in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}), {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}}: from 10th merit rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower, 10th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}) - written as "Sato deity". * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872|1=866}}), {{ill|lt=May 27|Q2587|WD=Q2587|1=May 27}}: from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) - written as "Sato deity". * Jōgan 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}), {{ill|lt=December 29|Q2862|WD=Q2862|1=December 29}}: from Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Sato deity". === Precincts === File:Sato Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sato Jinja Keidai.jpg|Shrine marker and precincts File:Sato Atago Jinja.jpg|Atago Shrine torii === References === * Explanatory board in the precincts * "Engishiki shrine of Kuji District, Ninomiya Sato Shrine" (origin text) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province|Q11481683|WD=Q11481683|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province}} {{神道 横}}{{Index of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}Satonomiya-chō 1052 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721|1=788}}) |本殿 = Hiyoshi-zukuri, copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Sato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sato Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sato Shrine''' (さとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * '''Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto''' (たちはやびおのみこと) This deity appears in the {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}}. The text says: "His name is '''Tachihayao-no-Mikoto'''; another name is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayafuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'''." In the fudoki, when local residents suffered from the deity's curse, Kataoka no Ōmuraji (grandson of {{ill|lt=Kunimo Ōkashima-no-Mikoto|Q99517269|WD=Q99517269|1=Kuninazu no Ōkashima no Mikoto}}), ancestor of the Kashima branch of the Nakatomi clan ({{ill|lt=Kashima clan|Q11677139|WD=Q11677139|lt=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji|1=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji}}), was dispatched by the court to perform rites. === History === === Foundation === The {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} contains an entry on Sato Shrine and states that the founding was in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721|1=788}}), when a shrine was built in Matsuzawa. In Enryaku 19 ({{ill|lt=800|Q23677|WD=Q23677|1=800}}), villagers petitioned and a great minister was dispatched; an oracle said, "Rather than dwell in an impure village, settle in the pure heights." The shrine was moved to Kabire-no-mine (Iriyonoma-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachi|Q633922|WD=Q633922|1=Hitachi, Ibaraki}}). In {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), because the mountain was steep and worship difficult, it was moved again to Konakajima (Satonomiya-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}). === Overview ===
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;background-color:#ffffff;white-space: nowrap;text-align:center" |+Changes in divine ranks of Sato deity and Shizuka deity{{神道・神社史料集成|200601|Sato Shrine}}.{{神道・神社史料集成|201601|Shizuka Shrine}}. !Year!!Sato deity!!Shizuka deity |- |846||10th merit rank

→ Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank||-- |- |874||Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank||-- |- |885||--||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Upper |- |{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}||Small||{{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}} |}
The first appearance in official histories ({{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q11420601|WD=Q11420601|1=National Histories}}) is in the entry for {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}} of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}) in the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}, where Sato deity of 10th merit rank is granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. It continued to rise, reaching Junior Fourth Rank, Lower in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a small shrine in {{ill|lt=Kuji District|Q1075212|WD=Q1075212|1=Kuji District, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}. At the end of the Six National Histories period, its divine rank was higher than {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q11663648|WD=Q11663648|1=Shizu Shrine}}, the district's {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, but in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as a small shrine, suggesting their relative influence reversed during that interval. From the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Satake clan|Q2975363|WD=Q2975363|1=Satake clan}}. According to this shrine's origin record, {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu (Iyo-no-kami)|Q11383430|WD=Q11383430|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} repaired the buildings in the {{ill|lt=Shōhei|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} era, and shrine tradition says {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshitoshi|Q11383467|WD=Q11383467|1=Satake Yoshikiyo}} moved it to the current location in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}), {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}}: from 10th merit rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower, 10th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}) - written as "Sato deity". * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872|1=866}}), {{ill|lt=May 27|Q2587|WD=Q2587|1=May 27}}: from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) - written as "Sato deity". * Jōgan 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}), {{ill|lt=December 29|Q2862|WD=Q2862|1=December 29}}: from Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Sato deity". === Precincts === File:Sato Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sato Jinja Keidai.jpg|Shrine marker and precincts File:Sato Atago Jinja.jpg|Atago Shrine torii === References === * Explanatory board in the precincts * "Engishiki shrine of Kuji District, Ninomiya Sato Shrine" (origin text) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province|Q11481683|WD=Q11481683|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province}} {{神道 横}}{{Index of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

From the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Satake clan|Q2975363|WD=Q2975363|1=Satake clan}}. According to this shrine's origin record, {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu (Iyo-no-kami)|Q11383430|WD=Q11383430|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} repaired the buildings in the {{ill|lt=Shōhei|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} era, and shrine tradition says {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshitoshi|Q11383467|WD=Q11383467|1=Satake Yoshikiyo}} moved it to the current location in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}), {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}}: from 10th merit rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower, 10th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}) - written as "Sato deity". * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872|1=866}}), {{ill|lt=May 27|Q2587|WD=Q2587|1=May 27}}: from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) - written as "Sato deity". * Jōgan 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}), {{ill|lt=December 29|Q2862|WD=Q2862|1=December 29}}: from Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Sato deity". === Precincts === File:Sato Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sato Jinja Keidai.jpg|Shrine marker and precincts File:Sato Atago Jinja.jpg|Atago Shrine torii === References === * Explanatory board in the precincts * "Engishiki shrine of Kuji District, Ninomiya Sato Shrine" (origin text) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province|Q11481683|WD=Q11481683|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province}} {{神道 横}}{{Index of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '茨城県常陸太田市里野宮町1052', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sato-jinja (Hitachiota)}} [[JA:薩都神社]] {{wikidata link|Q55541642}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sato Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sato Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}Satonomiya-chō 1052 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721|1=788}}) |本殿 = Hiyoshi-zukuri, copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Sato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sato Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sato Shrine''' (さとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * '''Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto''' (たちはやびおのみこと) This deity appears in the {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}}. The text says: "His name is '''Tachihayao-no-Mikoto'''; another name is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayafuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'''." In the fudoki, when local residents suffered from the deity's curse, Kataoka no Ōmuraji (grandson of {{ill|lt=Kunimo Ōkashima-no-Mikoto|Q99517269|WD=Q99517269|1=Kuninazu no Ōkashima no Mikoto}}), ancestor of the Kashima branch of the Nakatomi clan ({{ill|lt=Kashima clan|Q11677139|WD=Q11677139|lt=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji|1=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji}}), was dispatched by the court to perform rites. === History === === Foundation === The {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} contains an entry on Sato Shrine and states that the founding was in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721|1=788}}), when a shrine was built in Matsuzawa. In Enryaku 19 ({{ill|lt=800|Q23677|WD=Q23677|1=800}}), villagers petitioned and a great minister was dispatched; an oracle said, "Rather than dwell in an impure village, settle in the pure heights." The shrine was moved to Kabire-no-mine (Iriyonoma-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachi|Q633922|WD=Q633922|1=Hitachi, Ibaraki}}). In {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), because the mountain was steep and worship difficult, it was moved again to Konakajima (Satonomiya-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}). === Overview ===
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;background-color:#ffffff;white-space: nowrap;text-align:center" |+Changes in divine ranks of Sato deity and Shizuka deity{{神道・神社史料集成|200601|Sato Shrine}}.{{神道・神社史料集成|201601|Shizuka Shrine}}. !Year!!Sato deity!!Shizuka deity |- |846||10th merit rank
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower||-- |- |866||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank||-- |- |874||Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank||-- |- |885||--||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Upper |- |{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}||Small||{{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}} |}
The first appearance in official histories ({{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q11420601|WD=Q11420601|1=National Histories}}) is in the entry for {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}} of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}) in the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}, where Sato deity of 10th merit rank is granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. It continued to rise, reaching Junior Fourth Rank, Lower in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a small shrine in {{ill|lt=Kuji District|Q1075212|WD=Q1075212|1=Kuji District, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}. At the end of the Six National Histories period, its divine rank was higher than {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q11663648|WD=Q11663648|1=Shizu Shrine}}, the district's {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, but in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as a small shrine, suggesting their relative influence reversed during that interval. From the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Satake clan|Q2975363|WD=Q2975363|1=Satake clan}}. According to this shrine's origin record, {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu (Iyo-no-kami)|Q11383430|WD=Q11383430|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} repaired the buildings in the {{ill|lt=Shōhei|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} era, and shrine tradition says {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshitoshi|Q11383467|WD=Q11383467|1=Satake Yoshikiyo}} moved it to the current location in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}), {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}}: from 10th merit rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower, 10th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}) - written as "Sato deity". * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872|1=866}}), {{ill|lt=May 27|Q2587|WD=Q2587|1=May 27}}: from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) - written as "Sato deity". * Jōgan 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}), {{ill|lt=December 29|Q2862|WD=Q2862|1=December 29}}: from Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Sato deity". === Precincts === File:Sato Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sato Jinja Keidai.jpg|Shrine marker and precincts File:Sato Atago Jinja.jpg|Atago Shrine torii === References === * Explanatory board in the precincts * "Engishiki shrine of Kuji District, Ninomiya Sato Shrine" (origin text) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province|Q11481683|WD=Q11481683|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province}} {{神道 横}}{{Index of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '茨城県常陸太田市里野宮町1052', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sato-jinja (Hitachiota)}} [[JA:薩都神社]] {{wikidata link|Q55541642}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sato Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sato Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}Satonomiya-chō 1052 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721|1=788}}) |本殿 = Hiyoshi-zukuri, copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Sato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sato Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sato Shrine''' (さとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * '''Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto''' (たちはやびおのみこと) This deity appears in the {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}}. The text says: "His name is '''Tachihayao-no-Mikoto'''; another name is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayafuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'''." In the fudoki, when local residents suffered from the deity's curse, Kataoka no Ōmuraji (grandson of {{ill|lt=Kunimo Ōkashima-no-Mikoto|Q99517269|WD=Q99517269|1=Kuninazu no Ōkashima no Mikoto}}), ancestor of the Kashima branch of the Nakatomi clan ({{ill|lt=Kashima clan|Q11677139|WD=Q11677139|lt=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji|1=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji}}), was dispatched by the court to perform rites. === History === === Foundation === The {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} contains an entry on Sato Shrine and states that the founding was in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721|1=788}}), when a shrine was built in Matsuzawa. In Enryaku 19 ({{ill|lt=800|Q23677|WD=Q23677|1=800}}), villagers petitioned and a great minister was dispatched; an oracle said, "Rather than dwell in an impure village, settle in the pure heights." The shrine was moved to Kabire-no-mine (Iriyonoma-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachi|Q633922|WD=Q633922|1=Hitachi, Ibaraki}}). In {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), because the mountain was steep and worship difficult, it was moved again to Konakajima (Satonomiya-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}). === Overview ===
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;background-color:#ffffff;white-space: nowrap;text-align:center" |+Changes in divine ranks of Sato deity and Shizuka deity{{神道・神社史料集成|200601|Sato Shrine}}.{{神道・神社史料集成|201601|Shizuka Shrine}}. !Year!!Sato deity!!Shizuka deity |- |846||10th merit rank
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower||-- |- |866||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank||-- |- |874||Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank||-- |- |885||--||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Upper |- |{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}||Small||{{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}} |}
The first appearance in official histories ({{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q11420601|WD=Q11420601|1=National Histories}}) is in the entry for {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}} of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}) in the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}, where Sato deity of 10th merit rank is granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. It continued to rise, reaching Junior Fourth Rank, Lower in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a small shrine in {{ill|lt=Kuji District|Q1075212|WD=Q1075212|1=Kuji District, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}. At the end of the Six National Histories period, its divine rank was higher than {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q11663648|WD=Q11663648|1=Shizu Shrine}}, the district's {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, but in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as a small shrine, suggesting their relative influence reversed during that interval. From the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Satake clan|Q2975363|WD=Q2975363|1=Satake clan}}. According to this shrine's origin record, {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu (Iyo-no-kami)|Q11383430|WD=Q11383430|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} repaired the buildings in the {{ill|lt=Shōhei|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} era, and shrine tradition says {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshitoshi|Q11383467|WD=Q11383467|1=Satake Yoshikiyo}} moved it to the current location in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}), {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}}: from 10th merit rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower, 10th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}) - written as "Sato deity". * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872|1=866}}), {{ill|lt=May 27|Q2587|WD=Q2587|1=May 27}}: from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) - written as "Sato deity". * Jōgan 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}), {{ill|lt=December 29|Q2862|WD=Q2862|1=December 29}}: from Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Sato deity". === Precincts === File:Sato Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sato Jinja Keidai.jpg|Shrine marker and precincts File:Sato Atago Jinja.jpg|Atago Shrine torii === References === * Explanatory board in the precincts * "Engishiki shrine of Kuji District, Ninomiya Sato Shrine" (origin text) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province|Q11481683|WD=Q11481683|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province}} {{神道 横}}{{Index of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * '''Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto''' (たちはやびおのみこと) This deity appears in the {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}}. The text says: "His name is '''Tachihayao-no-Mikoto'''; another name is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayafuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'''." In the fudoki, when local residents suffered from the deity's curse, Kataoka no Ōmuraji (grandson of {{ill|lt=Kunimo Ōkashima-no-Mikoto|Q99517269|WD=Q99517269|1=Kuninazu no Ōkashima no Mikoto}}), ancestor of the Kashima branch of the Nakatomi clan ({{ill|lt=Kashima clan|Q11677139|WD=Q11677139|lt=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji|1=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji}}), was dispatched by the court to perform rites. === History === === Foundation === The {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} contains an entry on Sato Shrine and states that the founding was in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721|1=788}}), when a shrine was built in Matsuzawa. In Enryaku 19 ({{ill|lt=800|Q23677|WD=Q23677|1=800}}), villagers petitioned and a great minister was dispatched; an oracle said, "Rather than dwell in an impure village, settle in the pure heights." The shrine was moved to Kabire-no-mine (Iriyonoma-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachi|Q633922|WD=Q633922|1=Hitachi, Ibaraki}}). In {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), because the mountain was steep and worship difficult, it was moved again to Konakajima (Satonomiya-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}). === Overview ===
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;background-color:#ffffff;white-space: nowrap;text-align:center" |+Changes in divine ranks of Sato deity and Shizuka deity{{神道・神社史料集成|200601|Sato Shrine}}.{{神道・神社史料集成|201601|Shizuka Shrine}}. !Year!!Sato deity!!Shizuka deity |- |846||10th merit rank
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower||-- |- |866||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank||-- |- |874||Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank||-- |- |885||--||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Upper |- |{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}||Small||{{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}} |}
The first appearance in official histories ({{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q11420601|WD=Q11420601|1=National Histories}}) is in the entry for {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}} of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}) in the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}, where Sato deity of 10th merit rank is granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. It continued to rise, reaching Junior Fourth Rank, Lower in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a small shrine in {{ill|lt=Kuji District|Q1075212|WD=Q1075212|1=Kuji District, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}. At the end of the Six National Histories period, its divine rank was higher than {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q11663648|WD=Q11663648|1=Shizu Shrine}}, the district's {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, but in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as a small shrine, suggesting their relative influence reversed during that interval. From the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Satake clan|Q2975363|WD=Q2975363|1=Satake clan}}. According to this shrine's origin record, {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu (Iyo-no-kami)|Q11383430|WD=Q11383430|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} repaired the buildings in the {{ill|lt=Shōhei|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} era, and shrine tradition says {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshitoshi|Q11383467|WD=Q11383467|1=Satake Yoshikiyo}} moved it to the current location in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}), {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}}: from 10th merit rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower, 10th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}) - written as "Sato deity". * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872|1=866}}), {{ill|lt=May 27|Q2587|WD=Q2587|1=May 27}}: from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) - written as "Sato deity". * Jōgan 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}), {{ill|lt=December 29|Q2862|WD=Q2862|1=December 29}}: from Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Sato deity". === Precincts === File:Sato Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sato Jinja Keidai.jpg|Shrine marker and precincts File:Sato Atago Jinja.jpg|Atago Shrine torii === References === * Explanatory board in the precincts * "Engishiki shrine of Kuji District, Ninomiya Sato Shrine" (origin text) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province|Q11481683|WD=Q11481683|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province}} {{神道 横}}{{Index of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Architecture of Hitachiōta City]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '茨城県常陸太田市里野宮町1052', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sato-jinja (Hitachiota)}} [[JA:薩都神社]] {{wikidata link|Q55541642}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sato Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sato Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}Satonomiya-chō 1052 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721|1=788}}) |本殿 = Hiyoshi-zukuri, copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Sato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sato Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sato Shrine''' (さとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * '''Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto''' (たちはやびおのみこと) This deity appears in the {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}}. The text says: "His name is '''Tachihayao-no-Mikoto'''; another name is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayafuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'''." In the fudoki, when local residents suffered from the deity's curse, Kataoka no Ōmuraji (grandson of {{ill|lt=Kunimo Ōkashima-no-Mikoto|Q99517269|WD=Q99517269|1=Kuninazu no Ōkashima no Mikoto}}), ancestor of the Kashima branch of the Nakatomi clan ({{ill|lt=Kashima clan|Q11677139|WD=Q11677139|lt=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji|1=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji}}), was dispatched by the court to perform rites. === History === === Foundation === The {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} contains an entry on Sato Shrine and states that the founding was in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721|1=788}}), when a shrine was built in Matsuzawa. In Enryaku 19 ({{ill|lt=800|Q23677|WD=Q23677|1=800}}), villagers petitioned and a great minister was dispatched; an oracle said, "Rather than dwell in an impure village, settle in the pure heights." The shrine was moved to Kabire-no-mine (Iriyonoma-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachi|Q633922|WD=Q633922|1=Hitachi, Ibaraki}}). In {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), because the mountain was steep and worship difficult, it was moved again to Konakajima (Satonomiya-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}). === Overview ===
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;background-color:#ffffff;white-space: nowrap;text-align:center" |+Changes in divine ranks of Sato deity and Shizuka deity{{神道・神社史料集成|200601|Sato Shrine}}.{{神道・神社史料集成|201601|Shizuka Shrine}}. !Year!!Sato deity!!Shizuka deity |- |846||10th merit rank
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower||-- |- |866||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank||-- |- |874||Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank||-- |- |885||--||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Upper |- |{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}||Small||{{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}} |}
The first appearance in official histories ({{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q11420601|WD=Q11420601|1=National Histories}}) is in the entry for {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}} of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}) in the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}, where Sato deity of 10th merit rank is granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. It continued to rise, reaching Junior Fourth Rank, Lower in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a small shrine in {{ill|lt=Kuji District|Q1075212|WD=Q1075212|1=Kuji District, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}. At the end of the Six National Histories period, its divine rank was higher than {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q11663648|WD=Q11663648|1=Shizu Shrine}}, the district's {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, but in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as a small shrine, suggesting their relative influence reversed during that interval. From the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Satake clan|Q2975363|WD=Q2975363|1=Satake clan}}. According to this shrine's origin record, {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu (Iyo-no-kami)|Q11383430|WD=Q11383430|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} repaired the buildings in the {{ill|lt=Shōhei|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} era, and shrine tradition says {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshitoshi|Q11383467|WD=Q11383467|1=Satake Yoshikiyo}} moved it to the current location in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}), {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}}: from 10th merit rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower, 10th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}) - written as "Sato deity". * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872|1=866}}), {{ill|lt=May 27|Q2587|WD=Q2587|1=May 27}}: from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) - written as "Sato deity". * Jōgan 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}), {{ill|lt=December 29|Q2862|WD=Q2862|1=December 29}}: from Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Sato deity". === Precincts === File:Sato Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sato Jinja Keidai.jpg|Shrine marker and precincts File:Sato Atago Jinja.jpg|Atago Shrine torii === References === * Explanatory board in the precincts * "Engishiki shrine of Kuji District, Ninomiya Sato Shrine" (origin text) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province|Q11481683|WD=Q11481683|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province}} {{神道 横}}{{Index of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hitachiōta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}Satonomiya-chō 1052 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721|1=788}}) |本殿 = Hiyoshi-zukuri, copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|WD=Q2502|1=April 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Sato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sato Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Sato Shrine''' (さとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * '''Tachihayabi-no-Mikoto''' (たちはやびおのみこと) This deity appears in the {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}}. The text says: "His name is '''Tachihayao-no-Mikoto'''; another name is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayafuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'''." In the fudoki, when local residents suffered from the deity's curse, Kataoka no Ōmuraji (grandson of {{ill|lt=Kunimo Ōkashima-no-Mikoto|Q99517269|WD=Q99517269|1=Kuninazu no Ōkashima no Mikoto}}), ancestor of the Kashima branch of the Nakatomi clan ({{ill|lt=Kashima clan|Q11677139|WD=Q11677139|lt=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji|1=Nakatomi no Kashima no Muraji}}), was dispatched by the court to perform rites. === History === === Foundation === The {{ill|lt=Hitachi Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}} contains an entry on Sato Shrine and states that the founding was in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=788|Q30721|WD=Q30721|1=788}}), when a shrine was built in Matsuzawa. In Enryaku 19 ({{ill|lt=800|Q23677|WD=Q23677|1=800}}), villagers petitioned and a great minister was dispatched; an oracle said, "Rather than dwell in an impure village, settle in the pure heights." The shrine was moved to Kabire-no-mine (Iriyonoma-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachi|Q633922|WD=Q633922|1=Hitachi, Ibaraki}}). In {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), because the mountain was steep and worship difficult, it was moved again to Konakajima (Satonomiya-chō, {{ill|lt=Hitachiōta|Q838663|WD=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}}). === Overview ===
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;background-color:#ffffff;white-space: nowrap;text-align:center" |+Changes in divine ranks of Sato deity and Shizuka deity{{神道・神社史料集成|200601|Sato Shrine}}.{{神道・神社史料集成|201601|Shizuka Shrine}}. !Year!!Sato deity!!Shizuka deity |- |846||10th merit rank
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower||-- |- |866||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank||-- |- |874||Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank
→ Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank||-- |- |885||--||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Upper |- |{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}||Small||{{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}} |}
The first appearance in official histories ({{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q11420601|WD=Q11420601|1=National Histories}}) is in the entry for {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}} of {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}) in the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}, where Sato deity of 10th merit rank is granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. It continued to rise, reaching Junior Fourth Rank, Lower in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a small shrine in {{ill|lt=Kuji District|Q1075212|WD=Q1075212|1=Kuji District, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}. At the end of the Six National Histories period, its divine rank was higher than {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q11663648|WD=Q11663648|1=Shizu Shrine}}, the district's {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, but in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as a small shrine, suggesting their relative influence reversed during that interval. From the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Satake clan|Q2975363|WD=Q2975363|1=Satake clan}}. According to this shrine's origin record, {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu (Iyo-no-kami)|Q11383430|WD=Q11383430|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} repaired the buildings in the {{ill|lt=Shōhei|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} era, and shrine tradition says {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshitoshi|Q11383467|WD=Q11383467|1=Satake Yoshikiyo}} moved it to the current location in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=846|Q30454|WD=Q30454|1=846}}), {{ill|lt=September 8|Q2850|WD=Q2850|1=September 8}}: from 10th merit rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower, 10th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}) - written as "Sato deity". * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872|1=866}}), {{ill|lt=May 27|Q2587|WD=Q2587|1=May 27}}: from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) - written as "Sato deity". * Jōgan 16 ({{ill|lt=874|Q23843|WD=Q23843|1=874}}), {{ill|lt=December 29|Q2862|WD=Q2862|1=December 29}}: from Senior Fifth Rank, Upper, 7th merit rank to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower, 7th merit rank (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - written as "Sato deity". === Precincts === File:Sato Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sato Jinja Keidai.jpg|Shrine marker and precincts File:Sato Atago Jinja.jpg|Atago Shrine torii === References === * Explanatory board in the precincts * "Engishiki shrine of Kuji District, Ninomiya Sato Shrine" (origin text) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province|Q11481683|WD=Q11481683|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province}} {{神道 横}}{{Index of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sato Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hitachiōta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]][[Category:qq]]
